tools.name: pypsa
tools.description: Python for Power System Analysis. A DataFrame-native power system modeling framework with native support for DCPF, ACPF, DC OPF, SCUC, SCOPF, lossy OPF, and multi-period storage optimization.
strengths: Broadest native expressiveness: 8/11 pass across all target problem types including lossy DC OPF with LMP decomposition (A-10) and multi-period storage OPF (A-12)
DataFrame-native data model with zero-friction interoperability and clean extra_functionality callback for custom constraint injection (8/8 extensibility pass, B-1 through B-9)
weaknesses: No PSS/E format ingestion capability of any kind; Phase 2 FNM ingestion requires building a custom converter (P2-1)
Linopy model-building overhead dominates 10,000-bus OPF wall-clock: 302s model construction vs 6s solver time on DC OPF (C-3)

workarounds.description: Manual p_min_pu/p_max_pu bound construction to fix commitment schedule
Shadow price extraction from Linopy constraint duals instead of PyPSA accessor
Distributed slack blocked in OPF context; no formulation exists
GPL Levenshtein dependency replaceable with MIT rapidfuzz

tools.name: pandapower
tools.description: A mature Python power flow tool with a DataFrame-centric data model and embedded PYPOWER solver, excelling at steady-state network analysis.
strengths: Exemplary supply chain: BSD license, Sigstore provenance, embedded solver, all permissive dependencies, fully air-gap installable (F-1 through F-9)
Strongest maturity profile with dual institutional backing (Fraunhofer IEE) and confirmed European DSO/TSO production deployments (E-1 through E-7)
weaknesses: Four independent expressiveness failures: no unit commitment, no multi-period optimization, no lossy DC OPF, no SCOPF (A-5, A-9, A-10, A-12)
Hardcoded PYPOWER PIPS solver with no swap mechanism; 62s and 2.35 GB on 10,000-bus DC OPF; OOM on SCOPF (C-3, C-7, C-8)

workarounds.description: Sequential single-period DCOPF with manual ramp-rate bound manipulation
Manual PTDF/LODF + scipy.optimize.linprog bypassing pandapower OPF entirely
Monkey-patch PYPOWER internals for custom constraint injection

tools.name: gridcal
tools.description: A Python power system simulation tool with native LODF-based SCOPF and parameter-only solver swap via PuLP.
strengths: Native LODF-based SCOPF via consider_contingencies parameter and parameter-only solver swap via PuLP (A-9, C-7)
Fast DCPF/ACPF at 10,000-bus scale with first-class PTDF/LODF extraction (C-1, C-2, B-9)
weaknesses: Bus factor of 1, zero code review on merged PRs, and non-functional CI (only CodeQL runs; test suite never executed in automation) (E-2, E-3, E-6)
Two blocking formulation bugs: battery energy balance sign error (A-12) and distributed slack hardcoded off in OPF (A-11)

workarounds.description: Distributed slack hardcoded off in OPF; no workaround available
Battery energy balance sign error; no workaround available
Custom constraint injection via PulpLpModel.solve monkey-patch with internal naming conventions
Sequential snapshot OPF to avoid TapPhaseControl enum bug

tools.name: powermodels
tools.description: A Julia/JuMP-based power network optimization framework with a clean four-layer dispatch architecture enabling custom constraint injection in minimal code.
strengths: Clean four-layer dispatch architecture with two-level API enabling custom constraint injection in 4 LOC with dual extraction (B-1, B-6)
Machine-epsilon PTDF accuracy and fast DC OPF at 10,000-bus scale in 4s with 4-solver consistency (B-9, C-3)
weaknesses: No native SCUC: unit commitment is entirely outside the tool's design scope (A-5)
ACPF fails at 10,000-bus scale (Ipopt/MUMPS diverge); bus factor of 1 with primary author inactive for 12 months (C-2, E-6)

workarounds.description: Cyclic SoC constraint via manual JuMP injection
Distributed slack requires ~150 LOC custom PTDF-based JuMP OPF
SCOPF requires manual Benders cutting plane with LODF injection
Quadratic cost linearization required for 45.5% of generators at 10,000-bus scale

tools.name: powersimulations
tools.description: An NREL/DOE simulation-oriented optimization framework built on JuMP with built-in unit commitment formulations and a 5-layer package architecture.
strengths: Built-in unit commitment formulations (ThermalStandardUnitCommitment) with the cleanest separation of concerns among all evaluated tools (A-5, B-6)
NREL/DOE institutional backing with federal appropriations; strongest funding model and active development (E-4, E-6)
weaknesses: DC OPF at 10,000-bus scale requires 5 stacked fragile workarounds; SCOPF crashes with HiGHS OTHER_ERROR on 10,000-bus network (C-3, C-8)
0/10 official examples run unmodified, only 3/10 tests achievable from documentation alone, median 332 LOC per test (D-2, D-3, D-5)

workarounds.description: initialize_model=false + JuMP.optimize!() to bypass broken initialization
Manual JuMP BESS variables/constraints depending on PSI internal constraint naming
5 stacked workarounds: init bypass, linear costs, all-gens-available, unbounded branches, no hydro
Top-50 contingency selection + LODF constraint injection for SCOPF scaling

tools.name: matpower
tools.description: A MATLAB/Octave power system simulation toolbox with 25+ years of development. Included as a reference benchmark; excluded from primary ranking because the customer requires inspectable source code, which precludes the compiled MATLAB runtime.
strengths: Best-in-class supply chain: 9/9 pass, BSD-3, zero external dependencies, 100% pure MATLAB/Octave code, fully air-gap installable (F-1 through F-9)
Native PSS/E RAW parsing (psse2mpc), native PWL costs, and MOST built-in SCUC/multi-period/storage capabilities (P2-1, P2-2, A-5, A-12)
weaknesses: GLPK exit flag integration bug blocks SCUC result extraction, cascading to all 10,000-bus scalability tests being skipped (A-5, C-4)
Bus factor of 1 (97.6% lifetime commits from single maintainer) with funding in transition from academic grants (E-6)

workarounds.description: Benders iteration with LODF constraint injection; N-1 infeasible + MIPS numerical issues
Iterative loss injection external to OPF; cannot produce non-zero loss LMP components
GLPK MIP gap exit code mapped to failure; validated on bundled test case
